findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "stripe_connect_split" references sensitive artifacts (bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

description:
Check whether a domain can actually receive email (has MX records) via a real
DNS-over-HTTPS lookup — validate a customer/supplier email's domain before sending
or invoicing. An LLM can't know current DNS; this does the live lookup.

labels.name: vat_rate_by_country
labels.description:
EU VAT rates (standard / reduced / super-reduced / parking) for a country,
from the maintained ibericode/vat-rates dataset (fetched live, cached 24h) —
including which rate set was in force on an optional 'date' (YYYY-MM-DD) and the
names of regional exceptions (e.g. Canary Islands). Input e.g. 'DE'

labels.name: stripe_connect_split
labels.description:
Compute the Stripe Connect three-way split for one charge.

Returns what the buyer pays, what Stripe takes, what the platform nets (its
application fee), and what the connected seller nets — plus the platform's
effective take rate. fee_bearer: 'seller' | 'platform' | 'buyer' (who absorbs the
Stripe

labels.name: payout_reconciliation
labels.description:
Explain why a payout is less than sales: walk gross -> deductions -> expected,
and (if actual_deposit given) flag the unexplained gap (shortfall/surplus).

labels.name: reorder_point
labels.description:
Reorder point = lead-time demand + safety stock. If on_hand is given, returns
whether to reorder now and the days of cover remaining.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: check_email_domain · Private data: validate_eori, check_email_domain · Public sink: check_email_domain.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: check_email_domain · Private data: validate_eori, check_email_domain · Public sink: check_email_domain

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
